name: Data Health Panel
description: A Power BI custom visual that evaluates whether data can be trusted by showing a weighted 0-100 health score, four quality KPI tiles, and a plain-English diagnostic issue list.
shortDescription: Score completeness, freshness, uniqueness, and category validity directly inside Power BI reports.
whatItDoesTitle: A clear signal for the health of the data behind the report.
whatItDoesText: Data Health Panel evaluates selected fields across completeness, freshness, uniqueness, and category validity. It gives teams a concise health score, focused quality indicators, and diagnostic issues without turning the report page into a troubleshooting maze.
howToUseTitle: Add the visual, map the fields, review the signal.
useCasesTitle: Built for teams responsible for trusted data.
ctaTitle: Add a trust signal to your report data.
ctaText: Download the current Data Health Panel package for free. Documentation and Microsoft AppSource slots are prepared for the production release path.

fieldRoles.name: Fields to Check Completeness
fieldRoles.description: Fields where blanks or nulls reduce the completeness score.

fieldRoles.name: Fields to Check Uniqueness
fieldRoles.description: Identifier fields where repeated values reduce the uniqueness score.

fieldRoles.name: Field to Check Freshness
fieldRoles.description: A date field used to evaluate how old the dataset is.

fieldRoles.name: Fields to Validate Categories
fieldRoles.description: Category fields checked for blanks, low-frequency values, and fragmentation.

formattingControls: Show or hide the health score, KPI tiles, and issue list.
Adjust base font size for report layouts.
Configure fresh hours, stale hours, blank thresholds, low-frequency thresholds, fragmentation thresholds, and row limits.
Override good, warning, and bad score colors.

features.title: Weighted Data Health Score
features.does: Computes a 0-100 score from completeness, freshness, uniqueness, and validity using intent-driven Power BI field buckets.
features.matters: Gives report users a single trust signal before they interpret operational metrics.

features.title: Completeness Checks
features.does: Measures the average percent of non-blank values across fields selected for completeness.
features.matters: Makes missing values visible inside the Power BI report instead of hiding quality checks in a separate workflow.

features.title: Freshness Evaluation
features.does: Compares the latest selected date against fresh and stale hour thresholds.
features.matters: Helps IT and BI teams see when a Power BI report is relying on stale operational data.

features.title: Uniqueness Detection
features.does: Calculates distinct-value rates and flags duplicate identifier values, with row limits for distinct-count performance.
features.matters: Reduces duplicate-driven reporting errors such as double counting cases, tickets, or transactions.

features.title: Category Validity Review
features.does: Checks category fields for blanks, low-frequency categories, and high fragmentation.
features.matters: Surfaces operational taxonomy issues that can make filters, slicers, and status dashboards harder to trust.

features.title: Plain-English Issue List
features.does: Ranks diagnostic messages by severity and links issues to the related quality dimension.
features.matters: Turns quality scoring into actionable report feedback for analysts, BI developers, and operations teams.

steps: Import the Data Health Panel .pbiviz package into Power BI Desktop.
Add Data Health Panel to the report canvas.
Assign fields into the intent-driven buckets: completeness, uniqueness, freshness, and category validity.
Tune display, threshold, and color settings from the Power BI formatting pane.
Review the health score, KPI tiles, and diagnostic issue list directly in the report.

name: Pipeline Health Monitor
description: A Power BI custom visual that monitors pipeline run health by showing latest status, duration, SLA health, anomaly warnings, and duration trends for operational datasets.
shortDescription: Monitor pipeline status, duration, SLA health, and basic anomalies directly inside Power BI.
scoreFormula: Latest Duration > Average Prior Completed Duration x 1.5
whatItDoesTitle: A compact operational view of pipeline health inside Power BI.
whatItDoesText: Pipeline Health Monitor groups raw pipeline run rows by pipeline, identifies the latest run by maximum start time, and shows current status, run duration, optional SLA status, duration trend, and basic anomaly warnings without leaving the Power BI report canvas.
fieldRolesTitle: Field roles aligned to pipeline run telemetry.
howToUseTitle: Map run fields, then review pipeline health.
useCasesTitle: Built for teams watching operational pipelines.
ctaTitle: Add pipeline health monitoring to Power BI.
ctaText: Download the free Pipeline Health Monitor package and use the sample pipeline run data to test status, duration, SLA, and anomaly behavior inside Power BI.

fieldRoles.name: Pipeline Name
fieldRoles.description: Pipeline, job, workflow, or process name used to group run history.

fieldRoles.name: Run ID
fieldRoles.description: Unique run identifier. Duplicate Run IDs are deduplicated per pipeline.

fieldRoles.name: Status
fieldRoles.description: Raw run status text. Matching is case-insensitive and supports partial values such as SUCCESS, FAILED - timeout, and In Progress.

fieldRoles.name: Start Time
fieldRoles.description: Run start timestamp. Rows without Start Time are ignored.

fieldRoles.name: End Time
fieldRoles.description: Run end timestamp. Blank End Time is treated as Running.

fieldRoles.name: Expected Completion Time
fieldRoles.description: Optional SLA timestamp. When omitted, the SLA Status column is hidden.

formattingControls: Configure success, failure, and running status colors.
Show or hide the duration sparkline.
Enable compact mode for dense operational report layouts.

features.title: Latest Run Health
features.does: Groups runs by pipeline and identifies the latest run using the maximum Start Time.
features.matters: Gives Power BI users a current operational signal for each pipeline instead of forcing them to scan raw run logs.

features.title: Status Normalization
features.does: Interprets common success, failure, running, and unknown status values from raw text.
features.matters: Keeps pipeline health readable even when upstream tools use slightly different status labels.

features.title: Duration and Trend Sparkline
features.does: Shows latest duration and a compact sparkline for the last five runs by duration.
features.matters: Helps teams see whether a pipeline is slowing down before it becomes a visible reporting issue.

features.title: SLA Status
features.does: Displays On Time or Late when Expected Completion Time is mapped.
features.matters: Turns Power BI into a practical surface for monitoring operational reporting commitments.

features.title: Basic Anomaly Warning
features.does: Flags the latest run when its duration is more than 1.5 times the average prior completed duration.
features.matters: Surfaces performance drift directly inside reports without requiring a separate monitoring tool.

features.title: Duplicate Run Deduplication
features.does: Deduplicates repeated Run IDs per pipeline before evaluating the latest status and duration.
features.matters: Reduces noise from duplicate ingestion or repeated updates in pipeline telemetry tables.

steps: Import the Pipeline Health Monitor .pbiviz package into Power BI Desktop.
Add Pipeline Health Monitor to the report canvas.
Map Pipeline Name, Run ID, Status, Start Time, and End Time fields.
Optionally map Expected Completion Time to enable SLA Status.
Review latest status, duration, SLA health, anomaly warnings, and duration trends directly in the report.

name: Token Analytics Panel
description: A Power BI custom visual concept for understanding token usage, model consumption, and compute patterns across AI tools and workflows.
shortDescription: Give teams clearer visibility into token usage and AI compute inside Power BI.

features.title: Token Usage Breakdown
features.does: Summarizes prompt, completion, and total token usage by tool, model, team, workflow, or time period.
features.matters: Helps Power BI users see where AI compute is being consumed instead of treating token usage as an invisible backend detail.

features.title: AI Compute Clarity
features.does: Highlights usage spikes, high-consumption workflows, and model-level compute patterns.
features.matters: Gives data teams and operations teams better visibility into the drivers behind AI-tool cost and performance.

features.title: Cost Context
features.does: Connects token metrics with estimated spend, budgets, and usage thresholds.
features.matters: Makes AI adoption easier to govern when teams need to understand compute impact across products and internal tools.

steps: Connect token usage exports or telemetry tables to Power BI.
Add Token Analytics Panel to the report canvas.
Map token, model, tool, cost, and time fields.
Review usage patterns, compute spikes, and budget signals inside the report.
